zh.Wellesbourne Hastings, Warwickshire, Eng: 英國英格蘭瓦維其可爾維爾斯布恩 海斯廷斯
zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Peter de Montfort於大約1240年出生在英國瓦維其可爾波奧德瑟特時，他的父親Peter de Montfort36歲，母親Amice De Audley20歲。他於大約1265年與Matilda De Le Warre在英國瓦維其可爾波奧德瑟特結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1287年4月23日在英國英格蘭瓦維其可爾波奧德瑟特享年48歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Peter de Montfort于大约1240年出生在英国瓦維其可爾波奧德瑟特时，他的父亲Peter de Montfort36岁，母亲Amice De Audley20岁。他于大约1265年与Matilda De Le Warre在英国瓦維其可爾波奧德瑟特结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1287年4月23日在英国英格兰瓦維其可爾波奧德瑟特享年48岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Montfort
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French and Breton: variant of   Monfort   1. This surname is most common in Brittany and Haute-Savoie. See also 2 below.
Altered form of Dutch Montfoort: habitational name from Montfoort in Utrecht. It is a cognate of 1 above (see Monfort 1) and also its altered form (or perhaps vice versa).
English (of Norman origin): very rare variant of   Mountford  , a cognate of 1 above.

variants.name: Monfort
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French/Haitian Jacques, Marcel, Monique, Wilner.

French and Breton: habitational name from any of numerous places called Montfort, from Old French mont ‘hill’ (see Mont 1) + fort ‘strong, impregnable’ (from Latin fortis). This surname is by far most common in Brittany. It is also found in Haiti. Compare Montfort 1, see also 2 below.
Altered form of Dutch Montfoort, a variant of Montfort 2 and also a cognate of the French name in 1 above and an altered form of its variant Montfort 1 (or perhaps vice versa).

variants.name: Monforte
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Salvatore, Carmela, Carmine, Rocco, Rosaria, Sal.  Spanish Carlos, Francisco, Luis, Medardo.

Italian and Spanish: habitational name from any of numerous places called Monforte, from Latin mons ‘mountain’ (genitive montis) + fortis ‘strong’, probably a reference to the strategic position of these locations.
English (of Norman origin): variant of   Montfort  . This form of the surname is rare in Britain.

nameMeanings.name: Peter
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the name of the best-known of all Christ's apostles, traditionally regarded as the founder of the Christian Church. The name derives, via Latin, from Greek petros ‘stone, rock’. This is used as a translation of the Aramaic byname Cephas, given to the apostle Simon son of Jona, to distinguish him from another of the same name ( Simon Zelotes ). ‘When Jesus beheld him, he said, Thou art Simon the son of Jona: thou shalt be called Cephas, which is by interpretation, A stone’ (John 1:42). According to Matthew 16:17–18, Christ says more explicitly, ‘Blessed art thou, Simon Bar-jona … thou art Peter, and upon this rock I will build my church’. The name has been in continuous use since the Middle Ages.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Peter de Montfort was born about 1240, in Beaudesert, Warwickshire, England, his father, Peter de Montfort, was 36 and his mother, Amice De Audley, was 20. He married Matilda De Le Warre about 1265, in Beaudesert, Warwickshire, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 2 daughters. He died on 23 April 1287, in Beaudesert, Warwickshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 48.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Peter de Montfort est né environ 1240, à Beaudesert, Warwickshire, Angleterre, son père, Peter de Montfort, avait 36 ans et sa mère, Amice De Audley, avait 20 ans. Il a épousé Matilda De Le Warre environ 1265, à Beaudesert, Warwickshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé le 23 avril 1287, à Beaudesert, Warwickshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 48 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Peter de Montfort(이)가 대략 1240년 영국 워윅실르 보데세르트에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Peter de Montfort(은)는 36세였고, 그의 어머니 Amice De Audley(은)는 20세였다. 그는 대략 1265년 영국 워윅실르 보데세르트에서 Matilda De Le Warre(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1287년 4월 23일 영국 잉글랜드 워윅실르 보데세르트에서 48세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Peter de Montfort родился около 1240 г. в местности бодесирт, варвиккшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Peter de Montfort было 36 лет, а матери, которую звали Amice De Audley, 20. Он женился на женщине по имени Matilda De Le Warre около 1265 г. в местности бодесирт, варвиккшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 2 дочери. Он умер 23 апреля 1287 г. в местности бодесирт, варвиккшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 48 лет.
